title: Fatdog64 runs as root - but podman will not!
body:
### Issue Description

Hi - very new to podman this will be a problem on my end.

Obtained a podman binary...
https://github.com/mgoltzsche/podman-static/releases/latest/download/podman-linux-amd64.tar.gz

rt-click installed it.

```
# podman --version
WARN[0000] The storage 'driver' option should be set in /root/.config/containers/storage.conf. A driver was picked automatically. 
podman version 4.6.1

# podman machine init
WARN[0000] The storage 'driver' option should be set in /root/.config/containers/storage.conf. A driver was picked automatically. 
Error: cannot run command "podman machine init" as root


Having created a podman user...

# su podman
podman$ podman machine init --disk-size 2
ERRO[0000] XDG_RUNTIME_DIR directory "/tmp/runtime-root" is not owned by the current user 


```

Fix ^^ that perms problem, another and another pop up. To the point that I am fairly sure I've got something badly wrong.

eg...

```
podman$ podman info
ERRO[0000] stat /root/.config/containers/storage.conf: permission denied 
podman$ 

```

Ideas welcome!

FD64 runs as root - would like podman to do the same!



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. On FD64-813 pull any podman binary release.
2. RtClick Install


### Describe the results you received

podman refuses to run as root

create a podman user ... that user cannot access /root/.config/...



### Describe the results you expected

Expected podman to run as root from a root terminal.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

Newb problems... I am new to containerisation... certain this is a config or installation problem my end.

comments.body:
podman machine is only designed to run as rootless.

It is not clear what you are trying to do but on linux you can use podman natively and do not need to create a machine VM. The standard podman will run as root.

@Luap99 - understood. Yes standard podman would be great! Didn't realise it _cannot_ run as root.

Podman isn't in the Fatdog repo so I pulled various versions until I found one that executed.

I'm not looking for anything fancy ... just want "podman machine init" to run!

 So - having created a podman user ...
 
```
 # su podman
 podman$ podman info
ERRO[0000] stat /root/.config/containers/storage.conf: permission denied 
podman$ 

```

I must have missed a configuration step?

As said before you can run podman as root just fine, what you cannot do is run `podman machine` commands as root.

Setting up a proper rootless environment is more involved. I have no idea what your environment looks like but maybe it is easier to start on something that is known to work.
